cards.title: Constant-power discharge scope

citation.ieee_reference: [M7] "Test methods for improved battery cell understanding," v3.0, "Constant power discharge test," pp. 25-27.
citation.title: Constant power discharge test
citation.linked_reference_markdown: [M7] G. Mulder, H. Popp, W. Kohs, K. Trad, K. de Craemer, W. Porcher, M. Montaru, M. A. Martinez, A. Marongiu, M. Oyarbide et al., "Test methods for improved battery cell understanding," White Paper, 2018, pp. 25-27.

key_takeaways: The chapter frames constant-power discharge as a way to map capacity and energy under application-like power demand.
Temperature dependence is part of the method scope rather than an optional afterthought.
limitations: The exact power matrix still has to be aligned to the target cell and application.
allowed_deviations: Power-rate and temperature matrix narrowing requires review.

cards.title: Constant-power discharge execution block

citation.ieee_reference: [M7] "Test methods for improved battery cell understanding," v3.0, "Constant power discharge test," pp. 25-27.
citation.title: Constant power discharge test
citation.linked_reference_markdown: [M7] G. Mulder, H. Popp, W. Kohs, K. Trad, K. de Craemer, W. Porcher, M. Montaru, M. A. Martinez, A. Marongiu, M. Oyarbide et al., "Test methods for improved battery cell understanding," White Paper, 2018, pp. 25-27.

key_takeaways: The method uses standard-cycle precharge, 60 min rests, constant-power discharge, and CC-CV recharge.
Each power level is repeated three times before moving on.
limitations: Sub-zero work depends on manufacturer charge restrictions.
allowed_deviations: Do not remove the standard precharge or three-repeat structure without explicit review.

cards.title: Constant-power discharge outputs

citation.ieee_reference: [M7] "Test methods for improved battery cell understanding," v3.0, "Constant power discharge test," pp. 25-27.
citation.title: Constant power discharge test
citation.linked_reference_markdown: [M7] G. Mulder, H. Popp, W. Kohs, K. Trad, K. de Craemer, W. Porcher, M. Montaru, M. A. Martinez, A. Marongiu, M. Oyarbide et al., "Test methods for improved battery cell understanding," White Paper, 2018, pp. 25-27.

key_takeaways: Energy, capacity, voltage trace, and heating behaviour are all part of the expected output family.
limitations: The chapter leaves reporting granularity to the project.
allowed_deviations: If the matrix is narrowed, keep the average and replicate logic explicit.

cards.title: DST scope and role

citation.ieee_reference: [M8] "Test methods for improved battery cell understanding," v3.0, "Dynamic stress test," pp. 28-30.
citation.title: Dynamic stress test
citation.linked_reference_markdown: [M8] G. Mulder, H. Popp, W. Kohs, K. Trad, K. de Craemer, W. Porcher, M. Montaru, M. A. Martinez, A. Marongiu, M. Oyarbide et al., "Test methods for improved battery cell understanding," White Paper, 2018, pp. 28-30.

key_takeaways: The chapter frames DST as an automotive-oriented dynamic discharge regime derived from USABC practice.
Profile scaling must be tied to a declared power basis.
limitations: The chapter does not remove the need for application-specific endpoint choice.

cards.title: DST execution block

citation.ieee_reference: [M8] "Test methods for improved battery cell understanding," v3.0, "Dynamic stress test," pp. 28-30.
citation.title: Dynamic stress test
citation.linked_reference_markdown: [M8] G. Mulder, H. Popp, W. Kohs, K. Trad, K. de Craemer, W. Porcher, M. Montaru, M. A. Martinez, A. Marongiu, M. Oyarbide et al., "Test methods for improved battery cell understanding," White Paper, 2018, pp. 28-30.

key_takeaways: The DST profile is 360 seconds long, repeated continuously with no rest between profiles.
The maximum transition time between steps is 1 second.
limitations: The power basis still depends on the selected scaling route.
allowed_deviations: Do not change the continuous 360 s execution logic without explicit review.

cards.title: DST outputs and endpoint logic

citation.ieee_reference: [M8] "Test methods for improved battery cell understanding," v3.0, "Dynamic stress test," pp. 28-30.
citation.title: Dynamic stress test
citation.linked_reference_markdown: [M8] G. Mulder, H. Popp, W. Kohs, K. Trad, K. de Craemer, W. Porcher, M. Montaru, M. A. Martinez, A. Marongiu, M. Oyarbide et al., "Test methods for improved battery cell understanding," White Paper, 2018, pp. 28-30.

key_takeaways: The chapter ties endpoints to rated-capacity removal for performance tests and 80% of rated capacity for baseline life-cycle tests.
High-speed logging on the order of 500 ms is part of the intended output quality.
limitations: The chapter does not prescribe a universal profile-following metric.
allowed_deviations: If an endpoint is changed, preserve why it changed.

cards.title: Drive-cycle test scope

citation.ieee_reference: [M9] "Test methods for improved battery cell understanding," v3.0, "Drive cycle," pp. 49-50.
citation.title: Drive cycle
citation.linked_reference_markdown: [M9] G. Mulder, H. Popp, W. Kohs, K. Trad, K. de Craemer, W. Porcher, M. Montaru, M. A. Martinez, A. Marongiu, M. Oyarbide et al., "Test methods for improved battery cell understanding," White Paper, 2018, pp. 49-50.

key_takeaways: The chapter links drive-cycle testing to life expressed in kilometres rather than only cycle count.
NEDC and WLTP are the named reference profile families.
limitations: The chapter gives only a compact procedural skeleton.

cards.title: Drive-cycle execution block

citation.ieee_reference: [M9] "Test methods for improved battery cell understanding," v3.0, "Drive cycle," pp. 49-50.
citation.title: Drive cycle
citation.linked_reference_markdown: [M9] G. Mulder, H. Popp, W. Kohs, K. Trad, K. de Craemer, W. Porcher, M. Montaru, M. A. Martinez, A. Marongiu, M. Oyarbide et al., "Test methods for improved battery cell understanding," White Paper, 2018, pp. 49-50.

key_takeaways: The execution skeleton is standard charge, 30 min pause, NEDC/WLTP profile, recharge, and repeat.
The chapter clearly treats the profile as non-constant-rate cycling.
limitations: The exact profile trace is external to the chapter.
allowed_deviations: Keep the charge-pause-profile-repeat loop visible when tailoring the actual trace.

cards.title: Drive-cycle outputs

citation.ieee_reference: [M9] "Test methods for improved battery cell understanding," v3.0, "Drive cycle," pp. 49-50.
citation.title: Drive cycle
citation.linked_reference_markdown: [M9] G. Mulder, H. Popp, W. Kohs, K. Trad, K. de Craemer, W. Porcher, M. Montaru, M. A. Martinez, A. Marongiu, M. Oyarbide et al., "Test methods for improved battery cell understanding," White Paper, 2018, pp. 49-50.

key_takeaways: The output family mirrors other cycling profiles but is interpreted against realistic drive duty and lifetime translation.
limitations: The chapter gives only minimal post-processing guidance.
allowed_deviations: If distance-mapping is added, keep the chosen mapping explicit.
